title: anthemis tomentosa subsp. peregrina
intro: Anthemis tomentosa subsp. peregrina, also known as downy chamomile or white daisy, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and calming effects, making it useful for treating digestive issues, fever, and restlessness. The health benefits of Anthemis tomentosa subsp. peregrina include its ability to soothe anxiety and insomnia, reduce menstrual cramps, and ease digestive discomfort. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
